candidate_problems.text: Can the notion of 'interpreter structures' be made non-metaphorical and quantitatively precise enough to explain temporal asymmetry beyond standard thermodynamics? The key open problem is to specify what counts as an interpreter, what physical criteria distinguish mere irreversible dynamics from record-using/control-bearing systems, and whether such structures add explanatory power or just redescribe entropy increase and memory formation.

What is the causal/explanatory relation between the low-entropy boundary condition and the emergence of records, causation, memory, and retarded radiation: are layered knowledge-bearing systems genuinely necessary to make the arrow of time 'legible,' or are they only late products of an arrow already fully fixed by cosmological initial conditions? This tension matters because the conjecture risks conflating the origin of the arrow with the way embedded agents represent and exploit it.

Why did the universe begin in a state so exceptionally suited to the growth of irreversible structure and knowledge, and can that specialness be explained without smuggling in teleology or anthropic selection? This is the deepest unresolved question inherited by the conjecture: it relocates the arrow-of-time problem to the origin and measure of low-entropy initial conditions, but does not yet explain whether such conditions are dynamically selected, typical in a larger cosmology, or brute facts.
